title: Modular shipping base
abstract: A re-usable, returnable, stackable, modular shipping base 100 that incorporates replaceable components to make the base relatively quick and cost effective to repair. The shipping base 100 is formed from a number of stamped or cut sheet materials (e.g., sheet metal) that are then folded and attached to one another using a tab system. When a component is damaged, the damaged component may be removed from the base and replaced with another mass produced, stamped sheet material component. Components are attached to one another via adhesive, rivets, stitch welding, spot welding, or any other applicable process.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A shipping base for supporting product during storage and transportation of the product, said shipping base comprising: a top sheet configured to extend generally horizontally when the shipping base is assembled and in an upright position, said top sheet configured to support product for storage and transportation on a top of said top sheet; a center junction assembly configured to attach to a bottom of the top sheet and space the top sheet from a surface supporting the shipping base when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; an end frame assembly configured to extend along a side of the top sheet, and space the top sheet from the surface supporting the shipping base when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and a common comer configured to connect the end frame assembly to another end frame assembly of the shipping base when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 2. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly is a first end frame assembly configured to extend along a first side of the top sheet; the top sheet has a second side extending generally perpendicular to the first side; the shipping base further comprises a second end frame assembly configured to extend along the second side of the top sheet; and 

 the common comer is configured to attach to the first end frame assembly and the second end frame assembly. 3. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly is a first end frame assembly of a plurality of end frame assemblies of the shipping base; the plurality of end frame assemblies of the shipping base consists of four end frame assemblies when the shipping base is assembled; the common comer is a first common comer of a plurality of common corners of the shipping base; and the plurality of common corners of the shipping base consists of four common comers when the shipping base is assembled. 4. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the center junction assembly comprises a center junction; the center junction comprises a base plate and a plurality of spanners; the base plate comprises a main body extending along an axis and a plurality of protrusions extending generally perpendicular to the axis; a spanner is attached to the base plate at each junction between a protrusion and the main body and extends along each of the main body and the protrusion when the shipping base is assembled; and spanners are configured to attach to one another at abutting sides via end tabs when the shipping base is assembled. 5. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the center junction assembly comprises a plurality of center junctions joined together when the shipping base is assembled; the center junction assembly comprises a center junction; the center junction comprises a base plate; the base plate comprises a main body extending along an axis and a plurality of protrusions extending generally perpendicular to the axis; a protrusion of the plurality of protrusions comprises a raised end portion; the center junction is a first center junction of the plurality of center junctions, the axis of the main body of the first center junction is a first axis, and the base plate of the first center junction is a first base plate; the center junction assembly comprises a second center junction of the plurality of center junctions; the second center junction comprises a second base plate; the second base plate of the second center junction comprises a main body extending along a second axis and a plurality of protrusions extending generally perpendicular to the second axis; a protrusion of the plurality of protrusions of the second base plate comprises a raised end portion; the raised end portion of the first base plate extends over and is attached to a protrusion of the plurality of protrusions of the second base plate when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and 

 the raised end portion of the second base plate extends over and is attached to a protrusion of the plurality of protrusions of the first base plate when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 6. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the center junction assembly comprises a center junction; the center junction comprises a spanner; the spanner comprises: a first side; a second side adjoining and extending generally perpendicular from the first side; a first upper tab extending horizontally from a top of the first side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a second upper tab extending horizontally from a top of the second side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a first lower tab extending horizontally from a bottom of the first side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and a second lower tab extending horizontally from a bottom of the second side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; the first upper tab and the second upper tab extend inwardly toward one another; the first lower tab and the second lower tab extend inwardly toward one another; and the first side, second side, first upper tab, second upper tab, first lower tab, and second lower tab are integrally formed with one another by stamping a blank from sheet metal, folding the first upper tab and second upper tab, folding the second lower tab and the first lower tab, and 

bending the blank such that the first upper tab and second upper tab are brought closer to one another. 7. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the center junction assembly comprises a center junction; the center junction comprises a spanner; the spanner comprises: a first side; a second side adjoining and extending generally perpendicular from the first side; a first upper tab extending horizontally from a top of the first side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a second upper tab extending horizontally from a top of the second side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a first lower tab extending horizontally from a bottom of the first side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and a second lower tab extending horizontally from a bottom of the second side when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; the first upper tab and the second upper tab extend inwardly toward one another; the first lower tab and the second lower tab extend inwardly toward one another; the first upper tab and the second upper tab are attached to the top plate when the shipping base is assembled; and the first lower tab and the second lower tab are attached to a base plate of the center junction when the shipping base is assembled. 8. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the top sheet comprises a tab configured to contact a top of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; the tab extends over the end frame assembly above a fork hole in the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position, said fork hole configured to receive a fork of a fork lift lifting the shipping base from the surface supporting the shipping base. 9. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the top sheet comprises a tab configured to contact a top rail of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; the tab extends over the end frame assembly above a fork hole in a side rail of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position, said fork hole configured to receive a fork of a fork lift lifting the shipping base from the surface supporting the shipping base; and the top sheet is attached to the top rail of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled. 10. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the top sheet comprises a top plate; the top plate is generally rectangular such that the top plate has 4 sides; the top plate has tabs extending from 3 sides of the top plate; and 

 a side of the top plate without tabs includes a level portion and a recessed portion, the recessed portion having an elevation lower than the level portion when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 11. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the top sheet comprises two top plates; each top plate is generally rectangular such that the top plate has 4 sides; each top plate has tabs extending from 3 sides of the top plate; a side of each top plate without tabs includes a level portion and a recessed portion, the recessed portion having an elevation lower than the level portion when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and the level portion of one of the top plates is attached to the recessed portion of the other top plate and vice versa when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 12. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly comprises: a top rail extending generally horizontally and configured to attach to the bottom of the top sheet when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a side rail extending generally downward from the top rail when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and a bottom rail extending generally horizontally from the side rail below the top rail when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 13. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly comprises: a top rail; a side rail; and a bottom rail; the top rail, side rail, and bottom rail are integrally formed; the top rail is formed by folding the top rail from the side rail along a top edge of the side rail; the bottom rail is formed by folding the bottom rail from the side rail along a bottom edge of the side rail; and the top rail and bottom rail each extend generally horizontally from the side rail in the same direction such that the top rail is over the bottom rail when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 14. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly comprises: a top rail; a side rail; a bottom rail; and a fork channel; the fork channel is configured to attach to the side rail, top rail, and bottom rail when the shipping base is assembled; and 

 the fork channel is configured to attach to the center junction assembly and the top sheet when the shipping base is assembled. 15. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly comprises: a fork channel; the fork channel comprises: a bottom configured to extend generally horizontally when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a pair of opposing sidewalls extending generally upwardly from the bottom of the fork channel when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a pair of top sheet tabs, each top sheet tab extending generally horizontally and outwardly from one of the sidewalls of the pair of opposing sidewalls; a pair of side rail tabs at a first end of the fork channel, said pair of side rail tabs configured to attach to a side rail of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled, each side rail tab of the pair of side rail tabs extending outwardly from a sidewall of the pair of opposing sidewalls; the bottom of the fork channel and the pair of opposing sidewalls of the fork channel extend beyond pair of top sheet tabs at a second end of the fork channel opposite the first end of the fork channel. 16. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly comprises: 

 a fork channel; the fork channel comprises: a bottom configured to extend generally horizontally when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a pair of opposing sidewalls extending generally upwardly from the bottom of the fork channel when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a pair of top sheet tabs, each top sheet tab extending generally horizontally and outwardly from one of the sidewalls of the pair of opposing sidewalls; a pair of side rail tabs at a first end of the fork channel, said pair of side rail tabs configured to attach to a side rail of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled, each side rail tab of the pair of side rail tabs extending outwardly from a side wall of the pair of opposing sidewalls; the bottom of the fork channel and the pair of opposing sidewalls of the fork channel extend beyond pair of top sheet tabs at a second end of the fork channel opposite the first end of the fork channel; the second end of the fork channel is configured to be received in the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled; the sidewalls of the pair of opposing sidewalls are configured to attach to spanners of the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled; the bottom of the fork channel is configured to attach to a base plate of the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled; and 

 the bottom of the fork channel comprises a raised section at the second end of the fork channel, said raised section at the second end of the fork channel configured to extend over and attach to the base plate of the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 17. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the end frame assembly comprises: a fork channel; the fork channel comprises: a bottom configured to extend generally horizontally when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a pair of opposing sidewalls extending generally upwardly from the bottom of the fork channel when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a pair of top sheet tabs, each top sheet tab extending generally horizontally and outwardly from one of the sidewalls of the pair of opposing sidewalls; a pair of side rail tabs at a first end of the fork channel, said pair of side rail tabs configured to attach to a side rail of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled, each side rail tab of the pair of side rail tabs extending outwardly from a sidewall of the pair of opposing sidewalls; the bottom of the fork channel and the pair of opposing sidewalls of the fork channel extend beyond pair of top sheet tabs at a second end of the fork channel opposite the first end of the fork channel; 

 the second end of the fork channel is configured to be received in the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled; the sidewalls of the pair of opposing sidewalls are configured to attach to spanners of the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled; the bottom of the fork channel is configured to attach to a base plate of the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled; and the bottom of the fork channel increases in elevation and the pair of opposing sidewalls decrease in height from the first end of the fork channel to the second end of the fork channel such that the second end of the fork channel is configured to extend over and attach to the base plate of the center junction assembly when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position. 18. The shipping base of claim 1 , wherein: the common comer comprises: a body portion formed of rectangular tubing such that the body portion has four sides and two ends, said rectangular tubing extending generally vertically when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and a pair of corner attachment brackets, each comer attachment bracket of the pair of comer attachment brackets attached to one side of a pair of adjacent sides of the four sides of the body portion; a pair of end frame connectors, each end frame connector configured to extend from a side of the pair of adjacent sides over each comer attachment bracket when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and 

 a comer attachment bracket of the pair of comer attachment brackets is configured to attach to the end frame assembly of the shipping base. 19. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the common comer comprises: a body portion formed of rectangular tubing such that the body portion has four sides and two ends, said rectangular tubing extending generally vertically when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position such that the two ends are a top end and a bottom end; and a pair of corner attachment brackets, each comer attachment bracket of the pair of comer attachment brackets attached to one side of a pair of adjacent sides of the four sides of the body portion; a pair of end frame connectors, each end frame connector configured to extend from a side of the pair of adjacent sides over each comer attachment bracket when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; a nesting cap configured to connect to the bottom end of the body portion, said nesting cap having a generally circular hole therethrough; and a comer attachment bracket of the pair of comer attachment brackets is configured to attach to the end frame assembly of the shipping base. 20. The shipping base of claim 1, wherein: the common comer comprises: 

 a body portion formed of rectangular tubing such that the body portion has four sides and two ends, said rectangular tubing extending generally vertically when the shipping base is assembled and in the upright position; and a comer attachment bracket attached to one side the four sides of the body portion; and the corner attachment bracket is configured to attach to the end frame assembly of the shipping base; the corner attachment bracket is C shaped and oriented such that the corner attachment bracket has surfaces configured to attach to a top rail, a side rail, and a bottom rail of the end frame assembly to which the common comer is attached when the shipping base is assembled; and the corner attachment bracket is positioned inward of the top rail, the side rail, and the bottom rail of the end frame assembly when the shipping base is assembled.
